name: remove-deadcode
tagline: Orchestrates parallel sub-agents to scan, LSP-verify, and auto-commit removal of dead code in a TypeScript project.
verdict: No secret access, network egress, or hidden instructions found. Main risk: it auto-deletes files and auto-commits each batch (git commit, rm) without a per-batch user confirmation step, relying only on typecheck + LSP re-verification as the safety net. It also depends on non-standard tool primitives (task(), LspFindReferences, run_in_background) from its sou

highlights.summary: Phase 4 has agents `rm` dead files and auto `git commit` per batch with no explicit user confirmation step before committing; changes are git-reversible but this is autonomous destructive action by de
No negative-trigger guidance (e.g. non-TypeScript/non-bun projects, repos without an LSP integration) despite the skill being tightly coupled to that stack.

Relies on non-standard, undeclared tool primitives (task(), LspFindReferences, run_in_background agent orchestration) specific to the source framework (oh-my-openagent) rather than stock Claude Code t
